name: leash-research

containerEnv.//proxy: Default egress route: the squid hostname-allowlist proxy started by init-firewall.sh. CLIs (aws, kubectl, gh, curl) honor these and so reach allowlisted platform endpoints regardless of IP rotation. Set in both cases so a tool that reads only one form still routes correctly.

//baked: post-create.sh is COPY'd into the image (see Dockerfile) because /workspace is now bound to the app/ subfolder — the repo's .devcontainer/ lives ABOVE that bind and is not visible inside the container.
//postStart: Re-arm the egress firewall on every container START (not just create). postCreateCommand runs once at creation; a plain restart (Docker/daemon/machine reboot) skips it, leaving squid dead and iptables wiped while HTTPS_PROXY still points at :3128 — proxied clients then fail with ECONNREFUSED. init-firewall.sh is idempotent, so re-running it here is safe.
